Want to learn about marmalade?

Marmalade is a sweet, tangy fruit preserve made from
the juice and peel of
citrus fruits boiled with sugar and water.
The well-known version is made from bitter orange, but
other citrus fruits such as lemons and limes can
also be used.
Bitter orange is mostly used in marmalade because of
its high pectin content, which gives a thick consistency to
its marmalade. In addition,
the balance of acid and pectin is needed for consistency.
Fruits with low pectin have it added
to make the marmalade.
  • Sweet
  • Soft
  • Yummy!

Thanks for learning about marmalade

Learn more here on wikipedia.

Cheerio from Hanna
